title: Design + implement PR-based sync (corvee pr) — resolve same-repo task-commit churn
description:
Currently 'corvee sync' is direct-push: pull --rebase then push to the configured remote/branch (usually main). For a solo, single-repo workflow this is fine. For an open-source project — where corvee itself is about to live — this model has two failure modes:

1. Every claim/heartbeat/done writes a commit and pushes it. On a busy backlog that's dozens of micro-commits per day, all mixed into main's history alongside real code changes. Code-review PRs become noisy (they carry .tasks/ updates they don't need) and main's commit graph reads like a bot's diary.

2. The current model assumes the agent has write access to main. OSS contributors fork-and-PR; they can't push to main. corvee has no PR-creation primitive — agents would have to shell out to 'gh pr create' themselves, which the SKILL.md doesn't teach.

This story is a design spike followed by an implementation. Three plausible models to evaluate (and one to pick):

A) Separate tasks repo. .tasks/ lives in its own git repo, .git-submoduled (or independent) from the code repo. corvee sync stays direct-push but only pushes the tasks repo. Pros: cleanest separation, no PR churn. Cons: two repos to clone, harder for newcomers, breaks the dogfood model where .tasks/ ships in this same repo.

B) Sidecar branch + auto-PR. corvee sync commits to a 'tasks-update-<ulid>' branch on the same repo, opens (or updates) a single rolling PR back to main. The PR squash-merges nightly or on a maintainer trigger. Pros: no main noise, reviewable. Cons: needs gh CLI / GitHub API; cron-style merge job.

C) 'corvee pr' as an opt-in alternative to sync. Default behavior unchanged. New verb: 'corvee pr [--title ...] [--base main]' creates a branch, commits any pending .tasks/ writes, pushes to origin (fork-aware), opens a PR via gh CLI. Agents that have write access keep using sync; OSS contributors use pr.

Deliverables: pick a model, document the reasoning in docs/spec-questions.md (new SQ entry), implement, ship a new SKILL.md section that teaches the right workflow to AI agents. If C is picked, add a new GitClient.Fork()/CreatePR() port pair so the implementation stays clean-architecture-clean. shell out to 'gh' under the hood — that's already the trust boundary git operations use.
acceptance_criteria: Spike outcome documented in docs/spec-questions.md (new SQ-NNN entry) with model + rationale
Chosen workflow implemented; tests cover both happy path and 'no .tasks/ changes to PR' edge case
skills/corvee/SKILL.md teaches the new workflow (when to sync vs when to pr)
Existing corvee sync behaviour preserved for users who don't opt in
Fork-aware behaviour: works when origin is a fork and upstream is the canonical repo
